title: Yaoundé Nsimalen International Airport

infobox.value: [Douala](/wiki/Douala_International_Airport "Douala International Airport"), [Garoua](/wiki/Garoua_International_Airport "Garoua International Airport"), [Libreville](/wiki/L%C3%A9on-Mba_International_Airport "Léon-Mba International Airport"), [Maroua](/wiki/Salak_Airport "Salak Airport"), [N'Djamena](/wiki/N%27Djamena_International_Airport "N'Djamena International Airport"), [Ngaoundéré](/wiki/Ngaound%C3%A9r%C3%A9_Airport "Ngaoundéré Airport")  **Seasonal:** [Jeddah](/wiki/King_Abdulaziz_International_Airport "King Abdulaziz International Airport"), [Medina](/wiki/Prince_Mohammad_bin_Abdulaziz_International_Airport "Prince Mohammad bin Abdulaziz International Airport")[[11]](#cite_note-11)

[Addis Ababa](/wiki/Addis_Ababa_Bole_International_Airport "Addis Ababa Bole International Airport"),[[13]](#cite_note-13) [Libreville](/wiki/L%C3%A9on-Mba_International_Airport "Léon-Mba International Airport")

(French: *Aéroport international de Yaoundé-Nsimalen*) ([IATA](/wiki/IATA_airport_code "IATA airport code"): **NSI**, [ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **FKYS**) is the second busiest and largest public airport in [Cameroon](/wiki/Cameroon "Cameroon"). The airport is located 27 kilometers (16 miles) south of the capital, [Yaoundé](/wiki/Yaound%C3%A9 "Yaoundé"), near Nsimalen in Cameroon's [Centre Region](/wiki/Centre_Region_(Cameroon) "Centre Region (Cameroon)").[[4]](#cite_note-Ccaa.aero-4)

[Jeddah](/wiki/King_Abdulaziz_International_Airport "King Abdulaziz International Airport"), [Medina](/wiki/Prince_Mohammad_bin_Abdulaziz_International_Airport "Prince Mohammad bin Abdulaziz International Airport")[[11]](#cite_note-11) |

Brussels Airlines' flight to Brussels from Yaoundé stops in Douala, but the airline does not have [eighth freedom rights](/wiki/Freedoms_of_the_air#Eighth_freedom_(consecutive_cabotage) "Freedoms of the air") on the Yaoundé and Douala sector.

**Yaoundé Nsimalen International Airport** (French: *Aéroport international de Yaoundé-Nsimalen*) ([IATA](/wiki/IATA_airport_code "IATA airport code"): **NSI**, [ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **FKYS**) is the second busiest and largest public airport in [Cameroon](/wiki/Cameroon "Cameroon"). The airport is located 27 kilometers (16 miles) south of the capital, [Yaoundé](/wiki/Yaound%C3%A9 "Yaoundé"), near Nsimalen in Cameroon's [Centre Region](/wiki/Centre_Region_(Cameroon) "Centre Region (Cameroon)").[[4]](#cite_note-Ccaa.aero-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yaound%C3%A9_Nsimalen_International_Airport&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Starting operations in 1991,[[5]](#cite_note-5) Nsimalen was built to supersede the old international airport in Yaoundé, which was getting absorbed by the rapid growth of the city. It was also too close to the oil deposits of SCDP ("Société Camerounaise de Dépôts Pétroliers" or *Cameroon Oil Storage Company*). This former international airport of Yaoundé, still known as [Yaoundé Airport](/wiki/Yaound%C3%A9_Airport "Yaoundé Airport"), now serves as an [airbase](/wiki/Airbase "Airbase") for the [Cameroon Air Force](/wiki/Cameroon_Air_Force "Cameroon Air Force").

In 2004, Nsimalen airport served 190,487 passengers. The airport has 14 direct flights to 11 countries. It is also the [focus city](/wiki/Focus_city "Focus city") for the most popular airline in Cameroon, [Camair-Co](/wiki/Camair-Co "Camair-Co"), successor to the defunct [Cameroon Airlines](/wiki/Cameroon_Airlines "Cameroon Airlines"). The airport's growth of international traffic is 4% per year. The government has set a goal of servicing 1.5 million passengers and 50,000 tons of cargo per year.[[4]](#cite_note-Ccaa.aero-4)

## Facilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yaound%C3%A9_Nsimalen_International_Airport&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Facilities")]

Yaoundé Nsimalen International Airport covers 3,065 acres (1240 ha) at an elevation of 694 m (2,276 ft), and includes one passenger terminal, one cargo terminal and 6 aircraft stands. Nsimalen airport currently has one runway: heading 01/19, 3,400 m (11,154 ft) in length, with a width of 45 m (147 ft),[[6]](#cite_note-azworldairports.com-6) which could accommodate a [Boeing 747](/wiki/Boeing_747 "Boeing 747").[[6]](#cite_note-azworldairports.com-6)

The airport is equipped with modern facilities and can be accessed through four gates. It has six parking facilities, two of which are for long term use. Inside the terminal there are 10 check-in desks, currency exchange office, post office, restaurants, tourist information centre, business centre, banks and car rental, [limousine services](https://www.camerounlimousine.com) and taxi services. Cargo facilities are equipped with a 1,839 m2 (19,794 sq ft) warehouse, twelve 747 Freighter docks, a transit zone, animal quarantine, health office, X-ray/handling/mechanical equipment and refrigerated storage.

## Airlines and destinations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yaound%C3%A9_Nsimalen_International_Airport&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Airlines and destinations")]

| Airlines | Destinations |
| --- | --- |
| [Afrijet](/wiki/Afrijet_Business_Service "Afrijet Business Service") | [Libreville](/wiki/Libreville_International_Airport "Libreville International Airport") |
| [Air Côte d'Ivoire](/wiki/Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire "Air Côte d'Ivoire") | [Abidjan](/wiki/Port_Bouet_Airport "Port Bouet Airport") |
| [Air France](/wiki/Air_France "Air France") | [Paris–Charles de Gaulle](/wiki/Paris%E2%80%93Charles_de_Gaulle "Paris–Charles de Gaulle") |
| [ASKY Airlines](/wiki/ASKY_Airlines "ASKY Airlines") | [Lomé](/wiki/Lom%C3%A9-Tokoin_Airport "Lomé-Tokoin Airport"),[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) [Malabo](/wiki/Malabo_International_Airport "Malabo International Airport"),[[9]](#cite_note-9) [N'Djamena](/wiki/N%27Djamena_International_Airport "N'Djamena International Airport")[[10]](#cite_note-10) |
| [Brussels Airlines](/wiki/Brussels_Airlines "Brussels Airlines")[1](#endnote_1) | [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels_Airport "Brussels Airport") |
| [Camair-Co](/wiki/Camair-Co "Camair-Co") | [Douala](/wiki/Douala_International_Airport "Douala International Airport"), [Garoua](/wiki/Garoua_International_Airport "Garoua International Airport"), [Libreville](/wiki/L%C3%A9on-Mba_International_Airport "Léon-Mba International Airport"), [Maroua](/wiki/Salak_Airport "Salak Airport"), [N'Djamena](/wiki/N%27Djamena_International_Airport "N'Djamena International Airport"), [Ngaoundéré](/wiki/Ngaound%C3%A9r%C3%A9_Airport "Ngaoundéré Airport")  **Seasonal:** [Jeddah](/wiki/King_Abdulaziz_International_Airport "King Abdulaziz International Airport"), [Medina](/wiki/Prince_Mohammad_bin_Abdulaziz_International_Airport "Prince Mohammad bin Abdulaziz International Airport")[[11]](#cite_note-11) |

| [Royal Air Maroc](/wiki/Royal_Air_Maroc "Royal Air Maroc") | [Casablanca](/wiki/Mohammed_V_International_Airport "Mohammed V International Airport")[[14]](#cite_note-14) |

Notes

**[^1](#ref_1)** : Brussels Airlines' flight to Brussels from Yaoundé stops in Douala, but the airline does not have [eighth freedom rights](/wiki/Freedoms_of_the_air#Eighth_freedom_(consecutive_cabotage) "Freedoms of the air") on the Yaoundé and Douala sector.
